title: Riot Platforms (RIOT) Stock Forecast 2026 and 2030: The $647M Revenue Miner Selling BTC to Build an AI Empire
text: Riot Platforms is positioned at a strategic inflection point as a company transitioning from pure-play bitcoin mining to vertically integrated infrastructure operations. Trading at approximately $18.24 per share with a market cap around $6.3 billion, the company reported record FY2025 revenue of $647.4 million while simultaneously recording a $663 million net loss. Operational Metrics: 15,680 Bitcoin holdings (down from treasury liquidation), 42.5 EH/s deployed hashrate, 3.0 cents/kWh all-in power cost, 5,686 BTC mined in FY2025. Strategic Pivot: The analysis emphasizes Riot's transition from pure Bitcoin mining to data center infrastructure. The company secured a 10-year AMD lease generating approximately $311 million in estimated contract revenue, with expansion options up to 200 MW total capacity. Valuation Thesis: Starboard Value, a major shareholder, values the AI data center opportunity at $9-21 billion, considerably higher than the company's current market capitalization, arguing that Riot's 1.7 gigawatts of available power-connected capacity represents underutilized infrastructure. 2026 Outlook: The article projects a range of scenarios from $9-$42+ depending on Bitcoin price movement, additional tenant signings, and data center revenue validation.
